title: Case 1: How to Create an Email Address in the Admin Panel
description:
This SOP outlines the steps for creating a new email address from the Admin Panel and customizing the user creation form as per organizational requirements.
Respond to Administrator and do necessary changes in admin panel.
Steps to Create a New Email Address
Login to Admin Account:
Access your Admin Email Account.
Click the profile icon on the top-right corner of the screen.
Select “Admin Page” from the dropdown.
Navigate to Single User Section:
On the left-hand menu, click “Single User.”
Select “Add User.”
Fill in User Details:
A form will appear with fields to input the user’s information.
Enter the required details such as:
Email ID
Domain
Mailbox Space
(Other optional details like Name, Mobile Number, etc.)
Submit the Form:
Once all required fields are filled, click “Create” or “Submit” to complete user creation.
Customizing the User Creation Form (Optional)
If there are fields that are not relevant for your organization and you wish to make them non-mandatory:
Under the “Single User” section, go to “Customize Add User Form.”
You can then select or deselect fields to set them as non-mandatory.
Note:
The following fields are always mandatory and cannot be made optional:
Email ID
Domain
Mailbox Space
All other fields can be toggled based on your requirements.

title: Case 2: How to Delete an Email Address in the Admin Panel
description:
To guide administrators on how to delete a user email account through the Admin Panel and how to track deletion activities for audit purposes.
======================================================================
Respond to Administrator and do necessary changes in admin panel.
Steps to Delete an Email Address
Login to Admin Account
Access your Admin Email account using valid credentials.
Click on the profile icon located at the top-right corner.
Select “Admin Page” from the dropdown menu.
Navigate to the User Management Section
In the left-hand menu, click on “Single User.”
Then click on “User Details.”
Search for the User
Use the search bar to enter the user’s email address or username.
Wait for the search results to load.
Delete the User
In the search results, locate the user you wish to delete.
On the right side of the user row, click on the delete icon (🗑️).
Confirm the deletion when prompted to permanently remove the user.
Audit & Activity Tracking
To check deletion logs or activity history:
Go to the left-hand menu and click on “MIS Reports.”
Select “Activity Report.”
Use the available filter options to:
Search by action type (e.g., deletion)
Filter by admin username
Filter by date range
Review the report to verify:
Which user was deleted
Date and time of deletion
Administrator who performed the action

title: Case 3: How to Change password of an Email Address in the Admin Panel
description:
To guide administrators on how to reset or change the password of a user's email account through the Admin Panel, including optional enforcement of password change on next sign-in.
======================================================================Respond to Administrator and do necessary changes in admin panel.
Steps to Change User Password
Login to Admin Account
Log in to your Admin Email account using valid credentials.
Click the profile icon located at the top-right corner.
From the dropdown, select “Admin Page.”
Navigate to User Management
In the left-hand menu, click “Single User.”
Then select “User Details.”
Search for the User
Use the search bar to enter the email address or username of the user whose password needs to be changed.
Wait for the search results to display.
Reset Password (Option 1: Direct Reset)
In the search results, locate the correct user row.
On the right side, click the Password icon (🔒).
A prompt will appear to enter the new password.
You can also select the checkbox for “Change password at next sign-in” if you want the user to set a new password after logging in.
Reset Password (Option 2: Through Edit Panel)
Alternatively, click the Edit icon (✏️) on the user’s row.
A panel will slide in from the right side.
In this panel, go to the second tab titled “Change Password.”
Enter the new password and confirm it.
(Optional) Select or leave unchecked the “Change password at next sign-in” checkbox based on whether the user should be forced to update the password after logging in.

title: Case 4: How to Give Sub-admin rights to an Email Address in the Admin Panel
description:
To guide administrators in assigning admin rights (full or limited) to other users, enabling them to act as sub-administrators with specific roles and responsibilities.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------Respond to Administrator and do necessary changes in admin panel.
Steps to Assign Admin Rights / Create a Sub-Administrator
Login to Admin Account
Log in to your Admin Email account using valid credentials.
Click on the profile icon located at the top-right corner.
From the dropdown, select “Admin Page.”
Navigate to User Management
In the left-hand menu, click “Single User.”
Select “User Details.”
Search for the User
Use the search bar to enter the email address or username of the user you want to assign admin rights to.
Wait for the results to populate.
Assign Admin Rights (Option 1: Via Admin Rights Icon)
In the user row on the right side, click the Admin Rights icon (🛡️).
A panel will appear with the option to assign:
Full admin rights (access to all features), or
Limited admin rights (e.g., only permission to add users).
Select the permissions based on the required role and confirm.
Assign Admin Rights (Option 2: Via Edit Panel)
Alternatively, click the Edit icon (✏️) on the user row.
A side panel will open from the right-hand side.
Navigate to the fifth tab labeled “Admin Rights.”
Choose between:
Full Admin Access, or
Custom Permissions (e.g., only add users, no delete/change password access).
Save changes to apply the assigned rights.
Examples of Sub-Administrator Roles
User1 → Sub-admin with only add user rights
(Cannot delete or change user passwords)
User2 → Sub-admin with add and delete user rights
(Cannot change passwords but can manage user accounts)
Additional Notes
You can create multiple sub-administrators with varied permissions as per your organizational requirements.
Sub-admins will only have access to the features specifically assigned to them.
Changes in permissions can be updated at any time using the same steps.

title: Case 5: How to Create an Alias for an Email Account
Case 6: How to Activate/Deactivate Email Account in the Admin panel.
Case 7: How to Resolve Login Failed [Error:V] message seen in Webmail, Due to IdProtect Security Feature.
description:
Resolving Login Failed [Error:V] Due to IdProtect Security Feature.
Purpose:
To explain the cause of the error "Login Failed [Error:V]" and guide both users and administrators on how to verify and approve login patterns under the IdProtect feature.
Respond to Administrator and do necessary changes in admin panel.
Background
The error "Login Failed [Error:V]" occurs when:
IdProtect (network login protection) is enabled for the domain.
The user tries to log in from a new or unrecognized network/IP.
The login is blocked until the new pattern is verified.
This security feature helps prevent unauthorized access and notifies both the user and the admin of any new login patterns.
Verification Methods
There are two ways to verify a login pattern:
✅ 1. User-Based Verification
Steps:
The user should check their alternate email address or registered mobile number.
Open the message received with the verification link and code.
Click the link and enter the code provided in the message.
Once verified, login will be allowed from that network.
✅ 2. Admin-Based Verification
Steps:
Admin logs in to the Admin Email Account.
Navigate to:
Admin Page → Other Services → Suspicious Login Report
Click on the “Notification Sent” tab (not the default tab).
Search for the affected user/email address and click Submit.
Review the login pattern details.
Click “Accept” to approve the new login pattern.
Important Notes
Once the pattern is verified, the same network/IP will be considered trusted.
Ensure that any verification (user or admin) is only done after confirming the legitimacy of the login attempt.
Admins can use this feature to monitor and control access on a per-user basis.

title: Case 8: How to Resolve Login Failed [Error B]: Not Authorized Due to IdProtect Security
Case 9: Resolving Webmail Login - Error Processing Request #101 (TFA Issue)
description:
To resolve Error #101 encountered during webmail login when Two Factor Authentication (TFA) is enabled, but the user's mobile number is missing or not configured.
Respond to Administrator and do necessary changes in admin panel.
Background
When TFA is enabled for a user, the system sends a 4-digit passcode to the user’s registered mobile number.
The user can only log in after entering:
Their email password
The OTP (passcode) sent to their mobile
If no mobile number is registered, login fails with:
“Error processing request #101”
This is a common issue and must be resolved by an administrator.
Steps to Resolve Error #101 by Adding/Updating Mobile Number
Admin Login
Log in to the Admin Email Account.
Click the profile icon on the top-right corner.
Select “Admin Page” from the dropdown.
Go to User Details
In the left-hand menu, click on “Single User.”
Then select “User Details.”
Search for the Affected User
Use the search bar to enter the user’s email address.
Wait for the user details to appear in the search results.
Edit User Information
Click the Edit icon (✏️) next to the user entry.
A panel will open from the right-hand side.
Update Mobile Number
Navigate to the “Personal Information” tab within the edit panel.
Locate the Mobile Number field.
Add or update the mobile number with a valid, active number.
Click “Save” to apply the changes.
User Login Post Update
Once the mobile number is updated:
The user will receive the OTP/passcode during their next login attempt.
The user must enter both the password and passcode to successfully log in.

title: Case 10: How to Create Email Accounts in Bulk
description:
To guide administrators on how to create multiple email accounts at once using the Bulk User feature in the Admin Panel.
Respond to Administrator and do necessary changes in admin panel
Steps to Create Email Accounts in Bulk
Login to Admin Account
Log in to your Admin Email Account.
Click the profile icon at the top-right corner.
Select “Admin Page” from the dropdown.
Navigate to Bulk User Section
In the left-hand menu, click on “Bulk User.”
Select “Add Bulk User.”
Download and Prepare the Sample CSV File
Click “Download Sample CSV” on the top-right corner.
Open the CSV and fill in user data as required.
✅ Mandatory Fields:
Email ID
Mailbox Space
Domain
⚙️ Optional Fields:
All other fields are optional and can be made non-mandatory via
Single User → Customize Add User Form.
Password: You can either mention the password of leave it blank for system to auto-generate the password
Upload the Completed CSV File
Go back to Bulk User → Add Bulk User.
Upload the prepared CSV file.
System Validation & Error Handling
Once uploaded:
If the CSV is valid, the bulk job will start automatically.
If there are invalid fields or missing mandatory data, the system will reject the file.
An email notification with error details will be sent to the logged-in admin email address.
✅ Action: Check your email inbox for any error notifications, correct the data, and re-upload if necessary.
Monitor Bulk Upload Status
Click on the “Status” tab beside the “Sample CSV” tab.
Here you can track:
Job status: Queued, Processing, Completed
Number of successful vs. failed entries
Error descriptions for failed entries

title: Case 11: How to Delete Email Accounts in Bulk
description:
To guide administrators on how to remove multiple email accounts at once using the Bulk User feature in the Admin Panel.
Respond to Administrator and do necessary changes in admin panel
Steps to Delete Email Accounts in Bulk
Login to Admin Account
Log in to the Admin Email Account.
Click the profile icon at the top-right corner.
Select “Admin Page” from the dropdown.
Navigate to Bulk User Section
In the left-hand menu, click on “Bulk User.”
Select “Delete Bulk User.”
Download and Prepare the Sample CSV File
Click “Download Sample CSV” from the top-right corner.
Open the CSV file and fill in the required user data.
✅ Mandatory Fields:
User ID (Email address without domain or as per format)
Domain
ForceDelete (Set to yes or true to confirm deletion)
⚠️ If any of these fields are missing or incorrectly formatted, the deletion process will be rejected.
Upload the Completed CSV File
Return to the Delete Bulk User section.
Upload your prepared CSV file.
System Processing & Email Notification
If the file is valid, the bulk delete job will be initiated.
Once the job is completed, the admin will receive an email notification summarizing:
Total number of accounts processed
Successful deletions
Any failures or errors
Monitor Bulk Delete Status
Go to the “Status” tab beside the “Sample CSV” tab to check job progress.
Status will indicate: Queued, Processing, or Completed.
Any failed records will include error messages for correction.
Important Notes
Deleting an email account is permanent — all mailbox data will be lost unless backed up beforehand.
Double-check each entry in the CSV to avoid accidental deletions.
Only the admin who performs the action will receive the confirmation email.
Webmail SOP Index
SOP for basis cases related to Webmail.

title: Case 1: How to Add a Signature in Webmail
description:
This SOP outlines the steps for creating a new email address from the Admin Panel and customizing the user creation form as per organizational requirements.
Respond to users and do necessary changes in their webmail account.
Steps to Add an Email Signature
Login to Webmail
Open your browser and go to your webmail login page.
Enter your email ID and password, then click Login.
Access Mail Settings
Once logged in, click on your profile icon at the top-right corner of the screen.
From the dropdown menu, select “Mail Settings.”
Add Signature
Inside Mail Settings, locate and click on “Add Signature.”
Enter your desired signature content in the editor (text, contact info, disclaimer, etc.).
You may also use formatting options like bold, italic, links, etc., if available.
Once completed, click “Save” to apply the signature to your outgoing emails.
Notes:
The signature will be automatically appended to every email you send.
You can come back and edit or remove the signature at any time by revisiting the same settings.

title: Case 2: How to Change Password of Your Email Account (Webmail)
description:
To help users securely change their email account password from within the webmail interface.
Respond to users and do necessary changes in their webmail account.
Steps to Change Your Email Password
Login to Webmail
Open your browser and go to the webmail login page.
Enter your email ID and password, then click Login.
Open Settings
After logging in, click on the Settings icon (located at the top-right corner of the page).
Change Password
In the settings menu, click on “Change Password.”
Enter your current password, followed by the new password.
Confirm the new password and click “Save” or “Submit.”
Password Tips:
Use a strong password that includes uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters.
Avoid using personal information or common patterns.
Do not share your password with anyone.

title: Case 3: How to Create a Rule in Your Email Account (Webmail)
description:
To help users create custom rules or filters in their email account to automatically manage incoming emails.
Respond to users and do necessary changes in their webmail account.
Steps to Create an Email Rule
Login to Webmail
Go to the webmail login page in your browser.
Enter your email ID and password, then click Login.
Open Settings
Click on the Settings icon at the top-right corner of the page.
Navigate to Rule Settings
Click on “Mail Settings.”
Select “Manage Email Rules.”
Create a New Rule
Click on “Add Rule” or “Create Rule.”
Define your rule criteria:
From (sender email address) / To (recipient address)
Subject (keywords in subject line)
Choose the desired action, such as:
Move to a specific folder
Auto-forward to another email address (Want a copy in this mailbox, Yes/ No)
Save the Rule
Click “Save” or “Apply” to activate the rule.
Example Use Cases:
Automatically move newsletters to a "Promotions" folder.
Forward all emails from a specific client to another address.
Send important alerts to a custom folder for easy access.

title: Case 4: How to Add Auto-Forwarding in Your Email Account (Webmail)
description:
To help users automatically forward incoming emails from their webmail account to another email address.
Respond to users and do necessary changes in their webmail account.
Steps to Add Auto-Forwarding
Login to Webmail
Open your browser and go to the webmail login page.
Enter your email ID and password, then click Login.
Access Settings
Click on the Settings icon at the top-right corner.
Navigate to Auto Forward Settings
Click on “Mail Settings.”
Then click on “Auto Forward.”
Configure Forwarding Options
Enter the email address to which you want to forward all incoming emails.
Choose one of the following options:
✅ Yes, leave a copy of the messages – A copy remains in your inbox.
❌ No, delete the messages once they have been forwarded – Messages are not retained in your inbox.
Verify Forwarding Address
A confirmation email will be sent to the forwarding address you entered.
The recipient must click the verification link in the email to complete the setup.
Once verified, auto-forwarding will become active.
Important Notes:
If you do not see the “Auto Forward” option under Mail Settings, your administrator may have disabled this feature for your account or domain.
You can modify or disable the auto-forward rule at any time through the same settings menu.

title: Case 5: How to Set Up Auto-Response in Your Email Account (Webmail)
description:
To help users set up an automatic reply (vacation responder) that sends a predefined message to incoming emails during a specific period.
Respond to users and do necessary changes in their webmail account.
Steps to Add an Auto-Response
Login to Webmail
Open your browser and go to the webmail login page.
Enter your email ID and password, then click Login.
Access Settings
Click on the Settings icon at the top-right corner of the screen.
Go to Auto-Response Settings
Click “Mail Settings.”
Then select “Auto Response.”
Enable Auto-Response
Toggle the Auto-Response option to ON/Enable.
Set Your Auto-Responder Message
Enter the message you want to be sent automatically (e.g., vacation notice, out-of-office reply).
You may include contact details or alternative emails if needed.
Define the Time Period
Set a start and end date for the auto-response to be active.
The system will automatically deactivate the response after the selected period ends.
Save Settings
Click “Save” to activate the auto-response.
Important Notes:
Auto-response will only be sent once per sender per day, even if they email multiple times.
If auto-response isn’t functioning, check with your domain administrator to ensure the feature is not disabled.

title: Case 6: How to Update Alternate Email ID and Mobile Number (Webmail)
description:
To guide users on how to update their alternate email ID and mobile number in their webmail account, which are used for password recovery and security verifications.
Respond to users and do necessary changes in their webmail account.
Steps to Update Alternate Email and Mobile Number
Login to Webmail
Open your browser and go to the webmail login page.
Enter your email ID and password, then click Login.
Access Settings
Click on the Settings icon located at the top-right corner of the screen.
Go to Profile Details
Click on “Mail Settings.”  And Select “Profile Details.”
Authenticate with Your Password
Enter your current email account password to access profile editing options.
Update Information
In the profile section, you will see fields such as:
Alternate Email ID
Mobile Number
Make the necessary updates to either or both fields.
Save Changes
After editing, click “Save” to apply the changes.
Important Notes:
If you do not see the "Profile Details" option under Mail Settings, it means your administrator has restricted access to this feature.
Make sure the alternate email and mobile number are valid and accessible, as they are used for:
Password reset
Two-Factor Authentication (TFA)
Suspicious login verification.

title: Case 7: How to Delete Emails in Bulk (Webmail)
Case 8: How to Recall an Email (Webmail Only)
description:
To guide users on how to recall (undo sending) an email that has been mistakenly sent to internal recipients, using the Recall Mail feature available in Webmail.
🔄 Steps to Recall a Sent Email
Login to Webmail
Go to your organization's webmail login page  and enter your credentials and click Login.
Open Sent Email
Click on the “Sent” folder in the left navigation pane and Open the email you wish to recall.
Click the “Recall Mail” Option
Just below the subject line, you will see multiple options:
Show full headers | Report Spam | Save copy to RCloud | View blocked images | Recall Mail
👉 Click on “Recall Mail” on the far-right side
System Will Process the Recall
The system checks if the recipient has already read the email.
If unread, the mail is recalled successfully.
If read, the recall fails, and you will be notified.
After initiating a recall, you will get a confirmation email with one of the following messages:
✅ “Mail has been successfully recalled.”
❌ “Mail is already read by the receiver — recall failed.”
📌 Important Conditions to Know
🔒 Recall works only for internal users (same domain, e.g., @yourcompany.com).
❌ Recall does not work for external domains.
🕒 Email can only be recalled if it is unread by the recipient.
📧 You will receive a status email confirming whether the recall was successful or failed.
✅ When Can You Use This Feature?
You accidentally sent an email to a wrong internal recipient.
The recipient has not yet read the email.
You are using the webmail interface (this feature is not available in mobile or third-party email clients).

title: Case 9: How to Sort Email with Filter Options in Webmail
description:
This SOP provides guidance on how to use the built-in filter options in Webmail to easily sort, search, and manage emails based on specific criteria.
✅ Steps to Apply a Filter
Login to your email account via Webmail.
On the Top bar, in left side, you will be Filter icon.
Click the Filter icon (funnel) at the top of the email list.
Select one of the options based on your need (e.g., “Unread” to see unread emails).
The email list will refresh and show results matching your selected filter.
📥 1. Unread
Description: Shows only the emails that have not been opened or read.
Use Case: Helps you focus on pending or new emails that require your attention.
🚩 2. Flagged
Description: Displays emails that you have marked with a flag for follow-up or importance.
Use Case: Useful to revisit important emails you've marked previously (e.g., pending approvals, critical updates).
📬 3. To Me
Description: Filters emails that were sent directly to your email ID (not just CC or BCC).
Use Case: Prioritize emails where you're the primary recipient and action may be expected from you.
👤 4. Mentions Me
Description: Shows emails in which you are mentioned (e.g., @yourname) in the message body.
Use Case: Quickly locate conversations or threads where you're being referred to or tagged.
📎 5. Attachment
Description: Displays only the emails that include one or more file attachments.
Use Case: Easily find emails containing documents, images, or other shared files.
📅 6. Calendar Invite
Description: Filters emails that are meeting invitations or calendar requests.
Use Case: Helpful when you want to view or respond to meeting requests quickly.
🗓️ 7. Date Filter
Description: Allows you to filter emails based on a specific date or date range.
Use Case: Ideal for locating emails received on or between certain dates (e.g., end-of-month reports, last week’s updates).

title: Case 10: How to Select and Move Emails in Bulk (Webmail)
description:
To guide users on how to move multiple emails at once from one folder to another using Webmail’s advanced search and bulk selection features.
🧭 Steps to Move Emails in Bulk
Login to Webmail
Go to the webmail login page and sign in using your email credentials.
Use the Search Box
In the top-right corner, click on the settings icon to open Advanced Search.
To apply filters (e.g., sender, subject, date), click on the Advanced Search icon (small funnel icon).
View Search Results
Emails matching your search/filter criteria will appear.
Select All Emails Matching Search
After search results appear, click the option:
“Delete all mails matching search criteria” located on the top-right side.
Click on “Move” Option
You will see“Move to Folder” icon (usually a folder icon or dropdown).
Choose the destination folder from the list.
Confirm Action
Type “Move”
Emails will be moved in bulk to the selected folder.
Wait for a confirmation message or system response before navigating away

Case 1: Sorry, no mailbox here by that name. (#5.1.1)

Case 17: CNAME_lookup_failed_temporarily._(#4.4.3)
Case 18: The user is not allowed to receive mails from you. Please check with recipient.
Case 21: You are not allowed to send mails to this domain. Please contact recipient's domain administrator

Case 23: Sorry, I wasn't able to establish an SMTP connection with <domain>”25 (#4.4.1) I'm not going to try again; this message has been in the queue too long.  where <domain> can be any recipient domain.
description:
This SOP explains the bounce error “Sorry, I wasn't able to establish an SMTP connection with <domain>:25 (#4.4.1) I'm not going to try again; this message has been in the queue too long.” where <domain> can be any recipient domain, which occurs when the sender mail server is unable to establish an SMTP connection with the recipient mail server.
Reason for Issue
The sender server is unable to complete the SMTP handshake with the recipient server.
Steps to Resolve the Issue
Step 1: Contact Customer Support
Raise an email request with customer support and share the complete bounce error message.
Step 2: Await Investigation
Wait for confirmation from the support team after they investigate the SMTP connectivity issue.
Notes
This is a server-level connectivity issue and cannot be resolved from the user’s side.
Email delivery will resume once the SMTP connection issue is resolved.

title: Case 24: The email you are trying to send is rejected due to Mail-Filtering policy . Please check with the administrator (#5.3.1)
CASE 25: The user is not allowed to receive mails with attachments of certain type/size. Please check with recipient.
